channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Long queues strain Spanish registry offices after migrant regularisation programme begins
item.description: Spain has experienced significant pressure on its public administration services following the launch of a large-scale migrant regularisation programme approved by the government. The initiative, whic...

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez criticizes Trump’s golfing during Iran conflict while raising concerns about his fitness for office
item.description: Representative Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez commented on former President Donald Trump’s continued golfing during a period of heightened tensions with Iran, offering a nuanced and somewhat unexpected pers...

item.title: Harry and Meghan begin privately funded visit to Australia with charity engagements
item.description: The Duke and Duchess of Sussex, Prince Harry and Meghan, have arrived in Australia for a four-day visit that blends charitable work with private engagements. This trip marks a clear departure from the...

item.title: Renewables Found to Be Over 50% Cheaper Than Nuclear Power in New Study
item.description: A new study on Denmark’s future energy system reveals that a combination of offshore wind and solar energy would cost about €46 ($54.20) per MWh, making renewables 53% cheaper than nuclear power in th...

item.title: Junet Mohamed Defends ODM’s National Reach Amid Rising Political Rivalries
item.description: Suna East MP Junet Mohamed has come out strongly to defend the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM), insisting it remains the only truly national political party in Kenya as the country slowly gears up fo...

item.title: Apple Shares Rise More Than 1,900% During Tim Cook's Tenure as CEO
item.description: Apple has been one of the strongest long-term performers in the market during Tim Cook’s nearly 15-year run as chief executive. According to Dow Jones Market Data, the company’s shares have climbed mo...

item.title: Bovi and Nomzamo Mbatha to Host 12th AMVCA, IK Osakioduwa Hands Over
item.description: The Africa Magic Viewers’ Choice Awards (AMVCA) don don enter new chapter as organisers don announce Nigerian comedian Bovi Ugboma and South African actress Nomzamo Mbatha as co-hosts for the 12th edi...

item.title: UK government assesses potential food supply risks linked to disruption in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The UK government is preparing for the possibility of food shortages in a worst-case scenario linked to the ongoing conflict involving Iran. Officials have drawn up contingency plans that consider how...

item.title: Iran yet to decide on new peace talks as ceasefire nears end
item.description: Iran has stated that it has not made a decision regarding its participation in a new round of peace negotiations with the United States. This announcement came as the US delegation was on its way to P...

item.title: Shooting at Teotihuacan Pyramid Site Leaves One Dead and Several Injured
item.description: One gunman attack happen for Mexico famous Teotihuacan archaeological site, where one Canadian tourist lose im life and six other people sustain gunshot injuries. The incident happen on top the Pyrami...

item.title: Israeli Strikes Result in Two Deaths as Hamas Clashes with Militia in Gaza
item.description: On Monday, Israeli strikes in Gaza killed two Palestinians in separate incidents, according to local health officials. One person died in an airstrike at Bureij camp in central Gaza, while another was...

item.title: Prolonged Inter-Clan Conflict Among Chimpanzees in Uganda Highlights Innate Aggression
item.description: For over a decade, a prolonged conflict has been occurring among chimpanzees in Uganda's Kibale National Park, specifically on the Ngogo hill. The feud is not driven by resources or ideology but appea...

item.title: James Dolan’s Surveillance Operations at Madison Square Garden Target Fans and Employees
item.description: James Dolan, owner of Madison Square Garden (MSG), has reportedly implemented a massive and invasive surveillance network in his venues, particularly at MSG and Radio City Music Hall. Since 2018, the ...

item.title: Rebel Wilson Faces Defamation Lawsuit from 'The Deb' Lead Actress in Sydney Court
item.description: Australian actress Rebel Wilson appeared in a Sydney court on April 20, 2026, for the start of a defamation trial brought by Charlotte MacInnes, the lead actress in Wilson's directorial debut film 'Th...

item.title: ExxonMobil invests $300 million early to advance Usan offshore expansion project in Nigeria
item.description: ExxonMobil don already put about $300 million, which be roughly 30 percent of the total $1 billion budget, into the Usan infill oil project for offshore Nigeria, even before the company formally appro...

item.title: Long-serving Morrisons manager dismissed after confronting aggressive shoplifter amid strict safety policies
item.description: Sean Egan, a 46-year-old supermarket manager from Wolverhampton, was dismissed by Morrisons after nearly three decades of service for physically intervening in a shoplifting incident. The घटना occurre...

item.title: US Advises Staff to Leave Abuja Embassy Over Security Worries in Nigeria
item.description: The United States has ordered non‑emergency staff and their families to leave the US Embassy in Abuja, saying the security situation across Nigeria is getting worse. The US State Department’s updated ...

item.title: US-Iran ceasefire talks stalled over sanctions, nuclear programme and Strait of Hormuz control
item.description: The ceasefire between Iran and the United States is facing significant obstacles, with Tehran refusing to join further peace talks after the US seized an Iranian-flagged oil tanker in the Gulf of Oman...

item.title: Patient-Derived Brain Organoids Offer Insights for Alzheimer's Treatments
item.description: Researchers at Johns Hopkins Medicine have developed lab-grown brain tissues, or organoids, derived from patients with Alzheimer's disease, which may help predict how individuals respond to commonly p...

item.title: Police hunt suspect after fatal shooting of Hillbrow street vendor
item.description: Gauteng police are investigating the fatal shooting of a street vendor, Tabeth Chidziva, who was killed in Hillbrow, Johannesburg, over the weekend. The incident took place at the corner of Wolmarans ...
